shared_properties: The rig follows the same art deco gold trim and deep-shadow base palette used by optical props and tower architecture.
Every frame is authored for transparent-background compositing over the tower tileset.
The surface geometry keeps a clean, measurement-friendly silhouette to support puzzle trust and evidence traceability.
differences: This asset emphasizes moving platform geometry and angular readability rather than beam interaction.
The nine-frame checkpoint sheet encodes visually stable negative, neutral, and positive balance states.
Wide plate silhouettes leave enough flat surface for players to judge object distance from the axle by eye.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the shadow survival loop, mirror and prism puzzle rules, scale targets, HUD placements, and Bevy runtime constraints for Umbra Scale.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the twilight art deco tower mood, silhouette contrast, gold trims, and hard-edge lighting style.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load balance_scale_rig.png during Loading and map frame indices to nine authored tilt checkpoints from -45 degrees through +45 degrees for deterministic interpolation.
runtime_notes: Use authored axle alignment marks so the visible pivot matches the actual physics origin used for torque calculations.
The center axle and both plates must remain readable while dynamic text labels for left and right weight values are overlaid nearby.
Keep edge decoration concentrated on the axle housing so the plate surfaces stay uncluttered for player, Echo, and block silhouettes.

context.usage: Shows the two-plate scale, tilt motion, and target-angle readability that gate door unlock progression.

readability_notes: Each authored frame keeps the axle perfectly centered so interpolation does not wobble.
Plate silhouettes remain legible beneath player and Echo bodies at 75% preview scale.
Gold trim highlights the target reading area without overpowering the gameplay text overlays.

acceptance_criteria: Atlas canvas is exactly 672x432 px for nine 224x144 frames with no frame gutter.
The axle center stays on one vertical line across all nine tilt checkpoints.
The -30, 0, and +30 degree frames remain distinguishable at 50% preview scale without textual labels.
